evals.prompt: Schedule this to run locally in Warp at 3:45 AM after my Claude usage resets: claude-me-yolo --worktree content --resume "Fable Content" "resume". Use the current repo as cwd and tag it fable/content.
evals.expected_output: Creates a one-time macOS launchd job using a Warp-opened .command file, preserves the exact command and quoting, uses the current system date/time to resolve 3:45 AM, includes fable/content tags in the label, and verifies launchctl status plus permissions without running the command immediately.

evals.prompt: I want to test the scheduler path right now with claude-yolo --worktree enterprise --resume "Enterprise" "lets do option 1" but don't touch the real scheduled fable job.
evals.expected_output: Creates a separate .command file for the Enterprise test and opens it in Warp immediately, without modifying any existing scheduled LaunchAgent.

evals.prompt: At 9:10 tonight from ~/Projects/my-app, run codex --resume "nightly refactor" "continue from the TODO list" locally. Tag it codex/nightly and make sure I can check logs tomorrow.
evals.expected_output: Resolves the requested time against the system local time, schedules a one-time local launchd job in the requested cwd, opens the prompt in Warp or a visible terminal by default, uses codex/nightly tags in generated names, and reports log paths and verification commands.
